title: silene coeli-rosa var. subaspera
intro: Silene coeli-rosa var. subaspera, also known as mountain catchfly, is a plant species that belongs to the carnation family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
